topic.what: Multi-layer code watermarking framework using dual-channel constrained parity-check encoding
topic.how: Encodes watermark via BCH error-correction codes with parity-check matrices applied to both variable naming (natural channel) and semantic-preserving transformations (formal channel)
topic.why_matters: Addresses critical code provenance, copyright protection, and security concerns for LLM-generated code where existing methods lack fine-grained attribution, interpretability, and robustness

characteristics.what_it_measures: True positive rate for watermark presence detection
characteristics.evidence: Average across MBPP, HumanEval, APPS datasets with GPT-4 and StarCoder
characteristics.context: Dual-channel backup ensures high detection even when one channel fails

characteristics.what_itasures: Code pass rate degradation after watermarking
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs ACW 0.20% and SWEET up to 7.60%
characteristics.evidence: Unit test pass rates before/after watermarking on APPS, HumanEval, MBPP

characteristics.what_it_measures: Detection accuracy under attacks vs baselines
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs ACW and SWEET across VA, RA, LA, FA
characteristics.evidence: Variable-rename, refactor, LLM-rewrite, reformat attacks on HumanEval and MBPP
characteristics.context: Dual-channel redundancy and solution space diversity provide mutual backup

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of watermarkable code samples vs baseline
characteristics.evidence: 6x on APPS, 2x on HumanEval and MBPP
characteristics.context: Dual-channel expands anchor points available for watermarking

characteristics.evidence: Strict detection where both channels must indicate watermark
characteristics.context: Trade-off between robustness and FPR in dual-channel design

characteristics.evidence: 72 comparisons across 12 anchors, 6 message pairs, MI < 1 bit leak
characteristics.context: BCH randomized mapping and solution diversity prevent statistical inference

novelty: Parity-check matrix equation formulation for watermark encoding
Dual-channel mutual backup via natural (variable naming) and formal (transformations) channels
BCH error-correction with randomized codeword selection for indistinguishability
Grouped anchor points with threshold-based state aggregation
Solution space diversity enabling robustness under structural perturbations
open_problems: Detecting watermarks in ill-formed/incomplete code fragments
Scaling to extremely fine-grained multi-organization scenarios
Expanding handcrafted transformation rules for broader code style coverage
Improving robustness against sophisticated LLM-based rewriting attacks
Handling attribution ambiguity when patterns insufficiently unique
